Report And Accounting Of Former Administratix
Off Calendar Nov-20-2006 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: David Hinds appearing for administrator Judia Young. Terence Heath appearing for John Young, Jr.. Case is settled. The former administrator has withdrawn her accounting and the parties have agreed to waive the accounting. Mr. Hinds asked the Court to include in the order that check going to Mr. Young will be make payable to both Mr. Young and his counsel Mr. Heath. Order for final distribution signed with the modification requested by Mr. Hinds. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Floyd Butler, CSR#2518. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

The former administrator will withdraw the accounting and the two beneficiaries will waive accounting. Matter is continued to 11/20. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Patty Hubble CSR#3058. PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-24-2006 Denied
MINI MINUTES: John Carr and David Hinds appearing for Judia Young. Terence Heath appearing for John L. Young, Jr.. On petition to admit the lost will, it is denied. As a matter of law, the will was not properly executed. It doesn't show the witnesses signing at the same time. Oral argument presented by both sides. Mr. Carr wants an evidentary hearing. The petition for entitlement is set for trial in September. Court is denying the probate of the lost will. The will on its face, it is not a valid executed will. Court will review the order before signing it. The former administrator's accounting is continued to 10/16. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Teena Paxton CSR#1558. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Off Calendar Jun-12-2006 Vacated

Off Calendar Jun-13-2006 Off Calendar

For Determination Of Entitlement To Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Terrance Heath appearing for John Young, Jr.. David Hines and John Carr appearing for Judia Young. The petition for entitlement and the petition for removal of administrator have the same issues. Both sides will need two trial days for their side. Court is referring those issues to the Presiding Judge for trial date. Order signed. Mr. Hines informs the court that a copy of the will with the witnesses's signature was found. He will file a petition for probate of lost will or in the alternative, to file an amended petition for probate of will. Court wants to continue this issue for status on will contest. Matter is continued to 7/24. The former administrator's report and accounting is also continued to 7/24. The 7/6 and 7/7 trial dates are vacated. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
